title: Ptyssiglottis virgata
taxonomy: Ptyssiglottis virgata is classified within the family Gesneriaceae, commonly known as the African violet family. It belongs to the order Lamiales, a group of flowering plants that also includes mint, lavender, and snapdragons. The genus Ptyssiglottis is native to Australia and the Pacific Islands, with Ptyssiglottis virgata being a species found in tropical regions. This species is a climber or twiner, with heart-shaped leaves and purple flowers. It is often used in horticulture and as an ornamental plant due to its attractive foliage and vibrant blooms.

Its leaves and roots are often used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems.
